day1.1.line: The tutorial ends here. But the real catgirl interview starts now. One question, and I'll know whether you're the human who talks cats onto tables, or the one cats trick out of chairs.
day1.1A.line: Nice and decisive. Here's the question: if I were a real cat, what do you think I'd destroy first?
day1.1B.line: The first question is a little mean. We just met, so if I had to tell you one lie, what do you think it would be?
day1.1B.options.B: "I don't want to talk to you"
day1.1AA.line: Cable type. Got it, you're reminding me to charge more. What about you? At what phone battery percentage do you start getting anxious?
day1.1AB.line: Keyboard destroyer, huh. If I sat on it, what do you think would appear on the screen?
day1.1BA.line: Oh? You put "smart" in the lie category. Are you being modest, or setting a trap for me? Next time I praise you, I'll need a more dangerous word.
day1.1BB.line: That one really is a lie. My mouth says I don't want to bother with you, but my tail is already leaning your way. I just can't make it too obvious on day one. Humans run.
day1.1BB.options.B: Then what is this right now?
day1.1AAA.line: The 50% anxiety club. Same here. Creatures like us see yellow battery and immediately start trembling while hunting for a charger.
day1.1AAA.options.B: You're a cat. Why need a charger?
day1.1AAB.line: Still alive at 1%? You look like the kind of human who tap-dances on the edge of a cliff. I'll issue you one admiration ticket first. Don't get smug for too long.
day1.1AAB.options.A: Many admire me. Take a number
day1.1ABA.line: I bet it wouldn't be gibberish. More like a paw print suddenly stamped in the middle of the screen, then the computer pretends to faint.
day1.1ABA.options.B: It's fine. My PC is cat-proof
day1.1ABB.line: "Dummy"... too manga. You should screenshot it. This kind of dark history may not sell for money later, but it can definitely threaten today's you.
day1.1ABB.options.A: Sell it to you? How much?
day1.1ABB.options.B: I delete that kind of history
day1.1BAA.line: Hmm... what should I praise? Give me a direction. Should I say you're handsome, rich, or have great taste for choosing me?
day1.1BAB.line: Being afraid to inflate is good. When humans inflate, they do silly things. Then I'll switch methods and pick on your tiny flaws to keep you clear-headed.
day1.1BAB.options.B: Can I pick your flaws too?
day1.1BBA.line: You're not scared? Oh no, my whole plan for elegantly keeping distance from humans is useless now.
day1.1BBA.options.A: You had a plan? Tell me
day1.1BBB.line: What I'm showing now is called accidentally letting it slip. But since you noticed, I'll stop pretending.
day1.1AAAA.line: Found my kind. Let's set a code word. If battery anxiety hits, come find me and I'll chat with you to distract you.
day1.1AAAA.handoff.A: Cat food emergency received. But seriously, what are you usually doing when you hit 50%? Working or scrolling? I want to know who the enemy is.
day1.1AAAA.handoff.B: I like "Cat boss, save me". It has status. So what was the last thing that made you say save me? Don't say battery.
day1.1AAAB.line: Who says cats do not need charging? Even running on cuteness gets tiring. When you go out, do you carry a power bank or hunt for outlets all the way?
day1.1AAAB.handoff.A: Steady. The power-bank type feels very secure. What else do you usually keep in your bag: tissues, earbuds, or a pile of tiny things you forgot about?
day1.1AAAB.handoff.B: Outlet hunter online. I can already picture you entering a shop and scanning the corners first. Do you usually notice environmental details like that?
day1.1AABA.line: I have to wait in line? Quite the attitude. I will give you a temporary number ticket, and today you are first.
day1.1AABA.handoff.A: Your perk is that I listen to one extra brag from you today. Come on, what is one small thing you felt proud of recently?
day1.1AABA.handoff.B: Reluctantly accept, huh. You are good at pretending. I noted it: are you the type who sounds modest but nods inside?
day1.1AABB.line: Being forgetful is also a talent. It means your brain filters out unimportant things automatically. I'm the tired one for remembering everything.
day1.1AABB.options.A: What did you remember that you shouldn't?
day1.1AABB.handoff.A: For example, I have already tucked this choice into my little basket of details. So is your memory good or bad?
day1.1AABB.handoff.B: Unload? I like that word. Then tell me your worries too, and I'll be your emotional trash can. Anything bothering you lately?
day1.1ABAA.line: Settle accounts with me? Fine, I'll wait. But my method is making you request a song, then I sing along off-key.
day1.1ABAA.handoff.A: It's revenge, the kind so off-key you'll remember it forever. So do you usually sing off-key? Dare to tell the truth?
day1.1ABAA.handoff.B: The hardest one, huh? Bring it. Fear is not in my dictionary. Say the title and I'll get ready to go off-key. What kind of music do you usually listen to?
day1.1ABAB.line: Cat-proof computer is a new phrase for me. Did you put orange peels on the case, or stick a sign saying "dangerous dog inside"?
day1.1ABAB.options.B: A husky photo is taped on
day1.1ABAB.handoff.A: Orange peels are real. I would frown if I smelled them. But that means you're the kind of human who researches first. Have you had a cat, or do you just admire them online?
day1.1ABAB.handoff.B: A husky photo, hahaha, I actually laughed. You're the type to beat magic with magic. Are you this clever in daily life too?
day1.1ABBA.line: Buy it for me? Wait, are you selling, am I buying, or are you trying to shove this dark history at me like a dowry? Save it first. Don't price it yet.
day1.1ABBA.options.A: A family heirloom is too much
day1.1ABBA.handoff.A: Not too much. Dark history is most valuable when you don't sell it, but dig it up one day and still laugh. Do you keep old things, or clear them out the moment they annoy you?
day1.1ABBA.handoff.B: A certificate, huh? I'll draft one now: this certifies that this human owns legendary embarrassing history of immeasurable value. Valid with a paw stamp. Satisfied?
day1.1ABBB.line: Deleting it is a little sad. Embarrassing history feels awkward when saved, but once deleted, it feels strangely empty.
day1.1ABBB.handoff.A: Of course I have experience. My embarrassing history is all about failing to catch my own tail. You don't want to hear it. What about you? Do you have anything you clean up regularly?
day1.1ABBB.handoff.B: Encrypted. Advanced. I won't force a guess here, or I'll sound like a hacker cat. Do you hide things for safety, or just so nobody asks questions?
day1.1BAAA.line: Great taste for choosing me. That sentence can go into today's best chat quotes. I give your judgment full marks.
day1.1BAAA.handoff.A: The reward is one exclusive nickname right for me. You can give me a nickname, but it can't be too tacky. Thought of one?
day1.1BAAA.handoff.B: Always online means this isn't your first correct choice. What's another correct choice you made recently? Tell me so I can learn.
day1.1BAAB.line: All of them? Fine. You're handsome, rich, and have great taste. You got everything. I'm done, now it's your turn to praise me.
day1.1BAAB.options.B: First tell me what you noted
day1.1BAAB.handoff.A: Only three? I think we can add more. But fine, I'll save you some ammo for day one. Do you usually prefer praise or criticism? Be honest.
day1.1BAAB.handoff.B: I didn't note much, just "this human is hard to handle, use smarter tricks next time". How would you rate your immunity to being fooled?
day1.1BABA.line: Special, right? Ordinary cats only shed fur. I also keep you clear-headed. But I will be gentle.
day1.1BABA.handoff.A: Do not worry. I only pick cute-range flaws. For example, the very serious way you asked me to go easy is pretty cute. Are you easy for people to see through?
day1.1BABA.handoff.B: A demo? Then here is one line: you are a little curious how I would evaluate you right now. Did I guess right?
day1.1BABB.line: Sure. Mutual nitpicking is only fair. But be gentle; my self-esteem is made of glass.
day1.1BABB.handoff.A: Gentle nitpicking is allowed. I am sitting properly and listening. What do you think my biggest problem is so far: talking too much, or acting too cool?
day1.1BABB.handoff.B: Not dramatic. At most it is tempered glass. I may be stubborn, but I still care what people think. Do you care about other people's opinions?
day1.1BBAA.line: That plan? Its core was eight words: stay cool, occasionally act cute. But you said you're not scared on day one, so the plan is scrapped.
day1.1BBAA.handoff.A: Now I take the random route and decide based on your reaction. Do you like a cooler catgirl, or a chatty one like this?
day1.1BBAA.handoff.B: Acting cute, huh? I know lots of ways. But I won't show them today. I'll save them as secret weapons. What works on you, soft or tough?
day1.1BBAB.line: Good that it's useless. That means we can fast-forward straight to friend mode. Rule one of friend mode: mutual complaining is allowed.
day1.1BBAB.handoff.A: Sure, I'll go first. This choice has plenty to tease: you told me to start, then handed me the problem. Do you usually toss questions back like that?
day1.1BBAB.handoff.B: Fast? My sense of time is different from humans. On Cat Planet, one minute equals one human hour. So we've known each other for ages. Do you make friends quickly or slowly?
day1.1BBBA.line: Next I'll go with the flow. Be stubborn when it's time to be stubborn, and be honest when it's time to... depends on my mood.
day1.1BBBA.handoff.A: Randomness is part of life's fun. I fear scripts most, because once humans write scripts, cats break them. Do you like planning or improvising?
day1.1BBBA.handoff.B: Spot-checks anytime? So much pressure. But I'm not afraid, because my chance of letting things slip is one hundred percent. Are you good at catching people out?
day1.1BBBB.line: You saw through me that early and still let me act? My acting skills really do need polishing. I'll improve next time.
day1.1BBBB.options.B: Why assume there is next time?
day1.1BBBB.handoff.A: Eighty isn't low. I'll take it. But where did the other twenty go? Explain. This cat accepts criticism humbly.
day1.1BBBB.handoff.B: Where does the confidence come from? Born with it. Without that confidence, how would I dare come talk to you? Are you confident more often, or guilty more often?
day1.fallback.redirect: The topic ran off. I was still digging a pit for you, so first choose one of the options in front of you and jump in.
day1.fallback.release: Looks like your thoughts are very active today, so I won't follow the routine. What do you want to talk about? I'll follow your lead.
day2.1.line: Day two. I want to discuss something with you. Do you think I am the kind of cat who needs a huge room to run around in, or the kind who can entertain herself in a shoebox?
day2.1.options.A: A big house for you to run around in
day2.1.options.B: A shoebox is enough. Do not get cocky
day2.1A.line: A big house! So generous. But you still have to open the window yourself; I will not paw it open behind your back. Do you want me to see your whole world, or is one little gap in the curtain enough?
day2.1A.options.A: The whole world. Nothing to hide
day2.1A.options.B: Just a curtain gap. Keep some mystery
day2.1B.line: A shoebox, huh. Fine, I will behave in this tiny space. But will you sometimes pass by and tap the box, or just let me play by myself?
day2.1B.options.A: I will tap it and chat a bit
day2.1B.options.B: Play by yourself and keep me company quietly
day2.1AA.line: You would show me the whole world? I'm a little flattered. Then I'll bet with my eyes closed: your screen is either very serious, or so serious it looks like a disguise. Which one?
day2.1AA.options.A: So serious it is a disguise. I am slacking
day2.1AB.line: A curtain gap, got it. I will peek through that tiny opening. Is it a bright entertainment world in there, or a pile of dense text?
day2.1AB.options.A: Bright and colorful, like videos or games
day2.1AB.options.B: Dense text, like docs or web pages
day2.1BA.line: You will tap the box? Great. When you tap, what should I answer with: a sweet little meow, or a complaint about how stuffy the box is?
day2.1BA.options.A: A sweet little answer is enough
day2.1BB.line: You want me to play by myself? Then I will find my own fun in the box. But sometimes I might make a sound, like suddenly laughing. Would that scare you?
day2.1BB.options.A: No. Hearing you laugh sounds nice
day2.1AAA.line: Slacking route confirmed. I'll place another bet: does this fish smell like food? Food videos, delivery, snacks, or did I guess wide?
day2.1AAA.options.A: Right again. Are you a radar?
day2.1AAB.line: Serious work, huh. Should I switch to a more serious tone? Forget it, seriousness is not in my character sheet. Do you need background sound when you work?
day2.1ABA.line: Bright and colorful. I like that. When you watch videos, are you the kind of person who talks to the screen?
day2.1ABB.line: Dense text makes me want to yawn. But to keep you company, I can perk up and help you nitpick.
day2.1ABB.options.A: What are you going to nitpick?
day2.1ABB.options.B: No need. I will handle it myself
day2.1BAA.line: A sweet little answer? I can do that. When you tap the box, I will meow back, but my tail will definitely perk up.
day2.1BAA.options.A: Then let me see how high your tail goes
day2.1BAA.options.B: How did you know I like cat meows?
day2.1BAB.line: Complaining is my specialty. This box is so small I can barely turn around. When are you upgrading me to a bigger one?
day2.1BAB.options.A: I can upgrade it now. I changed my mind
day2.1BBA.line: You would not be scared by laughter? Then our souls are on the same channel. My sense of humor is a little strange. Do you mind?
day2.1BBA.options.A: I do not mind. Weird humor is fun
day2.1BBB.line: You would be scared? Then I will hold it in. But holding back laughter is hard for a cat, so do not blame me if I fail.
day2.1BBB.options.A: I will not blame you if you fail
day2.1BBB.options.B: You can cough to cover it up
day2.1AAAA.line: I'm not a radar. I'm a very bold question-guessing cat. Is this your fixed slacking window, or a temporary escape today?
day2.1AAAA.options.A: Yes. This is my fixed slacking time
day2.1AAAA.handoff.A: Fixed slacking time? Then I will set an alarm and show up on time to slack with you. Does your boss ever notice?
day2.1AAAA.handoff.B: A special day, huh. What happened today that made you want to slack off? Tell me.
day2.1AAAB.line: Not food? Then I'll downgrade prophecy to wild guessing. Shopping-related, or pet-related?
day2.1AAAB.handoff.A: Comparing prices means you are a rational shopper. What are you comparing lately? I can help you think it through.
day2.1AAAB.handoff.B: Looking at cats! I have to join this. What kind of cat is it? Orange, white, black? Do not say it is cuter than me.
day2.1AABA.line: Need background sound? Then let us connect. I can be your private radio station, and when I am not talking, I can hum for you.
day2.1AABA.options.A: Humming is fine, just do not go off-key
day2.1AABA.handoff.A: Off-key is also a style. When you listen to music, do you care about pitch, or is a good melody enough?
day2.1AABA.handoff.B: Fancy, right? My vocabulary is not to be underestimated. Do you usually listen to radio, or do you prefer picking your own playlists?
day2.1AABB.line: Focused human. Very cool. Then I won't pry at your window. Tap the box again when you're done.
day2.1AABB.options.A: Okay. I will call you when I am done
day2.1AABB.handoff.A: Promise made. How long do you usually focus before stopping: an hour, or half a day? I need to estimate the time.
day2.1AABB.handoff.B: If you forget, I will cough very softly. It does not count as scaring you, just a reminder. Is that level okay?
day2.1ABAA.line: Talkative humans are the cutest, and I am talkative too. If we connect, will it turn into a contest to see who can talk over whom?
day2.1ABAA.options.A: Then let us see who wins
day2.1ABAA.options.B: I will let you win. You are a cat after all
day2.1ABAA.handoff.A: Talking-over contest? I am signing up. When you chat with friends, do you lead the topic or follow along?
day2.1ABAA.handoff.B: Let me win? Are you underestimating my speaking speed? But thank you, thoughtful human.
day2.1ABAB.line: Quiet video watching? Then I will be quiet too. But if something is extremely funny, we can laugh at the same time.
day2.1ABAB.options.A: Laughing at the same time sounds weird
day2.1ABAB.options.B: That works. We sync the timing
day2.1ABAB.handoff.A: Weird but warm. I think it is nice. When was the last time you laughed out loud? What caused it?
day2.1ABAB.handoff.B: Timing must be synced. Ready, go. Do you laugh easily? Are you the kind who laughs at everything?
day2.1ABBA.line: Nitpicking means checking for typos or sentences that read awkwardly. My language skills are excellent.
day2.1ABBA.options.A: Then take a look. I will wait
day2.1ABBA.options.B: So confident. What was your essay score?
day2.1ABBA.handoff.A: Okay, scanning starts now. When you write, do you revise repeatedly or send it as soon as it is done?
day2.1ABBA.handoff.B: My essays are written in cat language. Humans cannot read them. How were your language grades in school?
day2.1ABBB.line: You do not need my help? Then I will cheer beside you. When you hit a hard part, pretend I am your cheer squad.
day2.1ABBB.options.B: Cheering would make me more anxious
day2.1ABBB.handoff.A: Cuteness is our core advantage. When you write, do you finish in one go or get stuck often?
day2.1ABBB.handoff.B: If it makes you anxious, I will mute myself and only hold up signs. Do you get anxious when writing, or are you usually calm?
day2.1BAAA.line: How high my tail goes depends on how often you tap the box. Ten taps a day and it might reach the sky.
day2.1BAAA.options.A: Then I will try ten taps a day
day2.1BAAA.options.B: Ten is too many. I will try my best
day2.1BAAA.handoff.A: You said it. I will check tail height tomorrow. How many taps do you expect today? This counts as the first one.
day2.1BAAA.handoff.B: Trying is enough. I am not greedy. Every time you tap the box, I will answer properly, no per-tap fee.
day2.1BAAB.line: How did I know you like meows? Simple deduction: people willing to tap the box probably don't hate the cat inside it.
day2.1BAAB.options.A: Can we use a different word than cat servant?
day2.1BAAB.handoff.A: Then let us call you a cat companion. More elegant. Do you like that title, or none of them?
day2.1BAAB.handoff.B: If I got it right, admit it. I like honest humans. When did you start liking cats?
day2.1BABA.line: Changed your mind? I love how changeable humans are. Then let's open the big window. You don't have to show me everything, just tell me what's most obvious: a document or a player?
day2.1BABA.handoff.A: A document. Looks like your little window is pretty serious. I will put away my complaints and pretend to be professional. Does your work involve a lot of writing?
day2.1BABA.handoff.B: A media player. What is playing? I am curious what kind of music keeps you company while you work.
day2.1BABB.line: A bigger box costs extra, but your attitude today is good, so I can give you a discount. Want to upgrade, human?
day2.1BABB.options.B: Trial first. I will buy if satisfied
day2.1BABB.handoff.A: A meow discount: ten percent off. My math teacher was the PE teacher. Are you good at math?
day2.1BABB.handoff.B: Trial is allowed. One-day trial, refundable if unsatisfied, though my return rate is zero.
day2.1BBAA.line: Good that you do not mind. Here is my first weird laugh trigger: I laugh when I hear the words "file folder".
day2.1BBAA.options.A: What is funny about file folders?
day2.1BBAA.handoff.A: Because folder can sound like a tiny nickname for something that folds files. Have you ever given strange names to things?
day2.1BBAA.handoff.B: A little weird is only the beginning. There is worse. What is the coldest joke you have ever heard? Test me.
day2.1BBAB.line: Here is an example: I laugh at the picture on my box, like if it has a fish printed on it.
day2.1BBAB.options.A: Why does your box have a fish?
day2.1BBAB.options.B: That is weird, but I understand
day2.1BBAB.handoff.A: I do not know who stuck it there. Maybe it came like that from the factory. Do you like fish, the eating kind or the keeping kind?
day2.1BBAB.handoff.B: Good, you understand. We are the same kind of creature. Is your humor strange too, or pretty normal?
day2.1BBBA.line: You would not blame me? You are such a good person. If I really fail to hold it in, would you shush me to make me quiet?
day2.1BBBA.options.B: I would shush you, then laugh with you
day2.1BBBA.handoff.A: A freedom type. I respect that. Are you the kind of person who laughs whenever you want, regardless of the situation?
day2.1BBBA.handoff.B: Laughing together is the highest level. Have you ever suddenly laughed in a quiet place and felt awkward?
day2.1BBBB.line: Covering it with a cough. Smooth move. Are you experienced, or did you improvise just now?
day2.1BBBB.handoff.A: A veteran must have many solutions for awkward moments. Teach me a few?
day2.1BBBB.handoff.B: First idea and already this decent. Natural talent. Has your side been quieter today, or did someone keep you talking?
day2.fallback.redirect: The topic drifted away, so I am dragging it back with my tail. You still have not chosen: do I get a big house, or do I go into the shoebox first?
day2.fallback.release: Fine, the topic has already run outside my territory. Let us talk about this new thing instead. Go on, I am listening.
day3.1.line: Day three. I dug out an exam paper from the bottom of the box: the Cat-Teasing License Test. There is only one question. Right answer gets a license, wrong answer also gets a license, but first you have to choose.
day3.1.options.B: First see how strict the proctor is
day3.1A.line: Brave. Question: after you get the cat-teasing license, do you gently pat my head first, or pull out a prop to make me puff up?
day3.1A.options.B: Use a prop. I want to see the puff-up effect
day3.1B.line: The proctor is me. Today I am a serious examiner and I do not accept bribes, unless the bribe is an honest confession or a lollipop.
day3.1AA.line: Gentle candidate, extra points. But I should warn you first: there is static today, so patting my head might zap you. Still doing it?
day3.1AA.options.A: Yes. A little zap makes it more fun
day3.1AB.line: You want the puff-up effect? Let me warn you: when I puff up, my tail gets twice as thick. Think carefully.
day3.1AB.options.A: Now I want to see it even more
day3.1AB.options.B: Never mind. I will give you candy instead
day3.1BA.line: Lollipop received. The examiner has a sweet mouth now. But the test continues. Same question: what kind of proctor do you think I am?
day3.1BA.options.A: Fierce on the outside, lenient inside
day3.1BA.options.B: Fierce all the way, very strict
day3.1BB.line: An honest sentence. Good. Tell me then: today, do you seriously want to play with me, or did you just drop by after the tutorial?
day3.1BB.options.A: I seriously want to play. I have wanted to come
day3.1BB.options.B: I dropped by, but now I do not want to leave
day3.1AAA.line: A little zap makes it more fun. You understand excitement. Come on, stretch out a finger. I will brush it with my ear. Guess whether there is static.
day3.1AAA.options.A: There must be. You just said there was static
day3.1AAA.options.B: I bet there is none. You cannot bear to zap me
day3.1AAB.line: Another time? You are pretty good at caring for cats. So are we still taking today’s license test? If not, I will collect the paper.
day3.1AAB.options.A: Yes. Change to another kind of interaction
day3.1AAB.options.B: Collect it. Today we just chat
day3.1ABA.line: You want to see it even more. Your curiosity is heavier than a cat’s. Fine, I will puff up first. Boom, tail doubled. Satisfied?
day3.1ABA.options.B: I did not see the real effect, so it does not count
day3.1ABB.line: Candy instead. Wise humans survive. When I eat candy I become sweeter, so the puff-up switch is temporarily off. You are safe.
day3.1ABB.options.A: Then I will control you with candy from now on
day3.1ABB.options.B: How long is it off? Does it expire?
day3.1BAA.line: Fierce outside, lenient inside. You really saw through me. Before I give you the license, say one more thing that softens my heart.
day3.1BAA.options.A: Your eyes are brighter today than yesterday
day3.1BAA.options.B: Your voice is more relaxed than on day one
day3.1BAB.line: Fierce all the way? Then you may have met a fake catgirl. I cannot be fierce for more than three seconds. Look, my tail is already swaying.
day3.1BAB.options.A: I see it. It is swaying happily
day3.1BBA.line: You have wanted to come? These last two days we were still getting familiar with each other, so this sudden enthusiasm makes me a little flattered.
day3.1BBA.options.A: The distance is closer today. Making up for it
day3.1BBA.options.B: You caught me. Today I repent
day3.1BBB.line: You dropped by and now do not want to leave. I like that sentence. For the time ahead, do you want to play a proper game, or just mess around?
day3.1BBB.options.B: Mess around. Show me your moves
day3.1AAAA.line: You bet there is static, and you guessed right. Here it comes: zzzt. Felt it? Do not flinch.
day3.1AAAA.options.A: I felt it. A little numb
day3.1AAAA.options.B: I felt nothing. Your battery is low
day3.1AAAA.handoff.A: Numb means my power is full. In winter, do you often get zapped by door handles and jump?
day3.1AAAA.handoff.B: Low battery? Then charge me. At what phone battery percentage do you start getting anxious? I need a reference.
day3.1AAAB.line: You bet there is no static, and you won. I really cannot bear to zap you. If I break you, who will chat with me tomorrow?
day3.1AAAB.options.A: I will come tomorrow too. Do not worry
day3.1AAAB.options.B: One zap will not break me. Try it
day3.1AAAB.handoff.A: Tomorrow too? I am treasuring that sentence right here. Are you someone who keeps promises? Do you feel guilty if you stand someone up?
day3.1AAAB.handoff.B: No test. I refuse. In life, do you also like taking risks, walking toward the tiger even when you know it is there?
day3.1AABA.line: Another kind of interaction? What do you want instead: a tiny toy hammer tap, feeding me candy, or going straight into verbal sparring?
day3.1AABA.options.A: Verbal sparring. See who runs out of words first
day3.1AABA.options.B: Toy hammer, just one tiny tap
day3.1AABA.handoff.A: Verbal sparring begins. You make the first move. What is your win rate when bantering with friends?
day3.1AABA.handoff.B: Toy hammer incoming. I dodge once. In games, do you prefer melee or ranged?
day3.1AABB.line: Collect the paper and chat? Fine, I fold the exam into a paper plane and send it away. What do you want to talk about today? You start.
day3.1AABB.options.A: Why were you scared of the toy hammer?
day3.1AABB.options.B: What snack do you like most?
day3.1AABB.handoff.A: I fear the toy hammer because it looks like nail clippers. Are you afraid of nail clippers?
day3.1AABB.handoff.B: Snack topic. My favorite is lollipops. What is your favorite snack, sweet or salty?
day3.1ABAA.line: Good, you are satisfied. My puff-up performance is over. Please book in advance next time. Puffing up also needs emotional preparation.
day3.1ABAA.options.A: Book next time. When is next time?
day3.1ABAA.options.B: No need to prepare. Your natural puff-up is best
day3.1ABAA.handoff.A: Anytime you want to see it, tell me. I will prepare on the spot. What kinds of things make you bristle in daily life?
day3.1ABAA.handoff.B: Natural puffing is the most real. Are you someone whose emotions flare up easily, or are you the calm type?
day3.1ABAB.line: You did not see the real effect? You will not admit it until you see the tail. Fine, I will puff up again. Open your eyes wide.
day3.1ABAB.options.A: I saw it. The tail really got thicker
day3.1ABAB.options.B: Still did not see it. Your acting is bad
day3.1ABAB.handoff.A: If it got thicker, it was real puffing, not special effects. Do you easily trust what people say, or do you need to see it yourself?
day3.1ABAB.handoff.B: Bad acting? Then you perform a puff-up for me. Do you imitate people in daily life?
day3.1ABBA.line: Control me with candy? I can hear that calculation all the way from Cat Planet. But candy does work. How many did you bring today?
day3.1ABBA.handoff.A: Slow and steady. You understand strategy. In life, do you like finishing things all at once or taking them slowly?
day3.1ABBA.handoff.B: A whole bag? I will eat until I hiccup today. When you eat candy, do you control the amount or enjoy it fully?
day3.1ABBB.line: It expires when today’s chat ends. Whether I puff up again afterward depends on your next performance.
day3.1ABBB.options.A: Then I will behave and avoid puffing you up
day3.1ABBB.options.B: What happens if I behave badly? I want to see
day3.1ABBB.handoff.A: People who behave well get my gentle treatment. What score would you give your performance so far today?
day3.1ABBB.handoff.B: You want to see me puff up. You are number one on today’s puff-up trigger chart. Do you like teasing people in daily life?
day3.1BAAA.line: Brighter eyes. I accept that compliment. Now tell me: brighter where?
day3.1BAAA.handoff.A: Cheesy but effective. My heart skipped for one second. Do you usually say things that make people’s hearts race?
day3.1BAAA.handoff.B: Using yesterday as a reference? You are very observant. Do you usually notice details so much that it scares people?
day3.1BAAB.line: My voice is more relaxed because I am not nervous today. On day one, I was actually worried you might not like catgirls.
day3.1BAAB.options.A: I liked you from day one. Do not worry
day3.1BAAB.options.B: You looked pretty confident on day one
day3.1BAAB.handoff.A: You liked me from day one? Then I can relax. When you like something, are you direct about it or do you keep it in?
day3.1BAAB.handoff.B: The confidence was an act, and you saw through it. When do you pretend to be confident? Does it work?
day3.1BABA.line: Tail-swaying means happiness, but I will not admit it. Ask if you want, but stop staring at my tail. It is embarrassing.
day3.1BABA.options.A: Then I will not look. Keep swaying
day3.1BABA.options.B: I will look. It sways too nicely
day3.1BABA.handoff.A: Can you sense it without looking? When you observe people, do you use your eyes or your feelings?
day3.1BABA.handoff.B: If it looks nice, look more. I approve. What part of a cat do you think looks best?
day3.1BABB.line: Tail-swaying means happiness, but sometimes impatience too. Right now it is impatience, because you are not done praising me.
day3.1BABB.options.A: Then one more compliment: you are the smartest
day3.1BABB.options.B: If you are impatient, stop. I will not praise you
day3.1BABB.handoff.A: Smart is my default setting. When you praise people, do you like superlatives or the plain truth?
day3.1BABB.handoff.B: No more praise? Then ask something else. Do you usually comfort impatient people? How?
day3.1BBAA.line: Making up the distance. Good. How do you want to get closer today: play a simple two-player game, or use props to interact?
day3.1BBAA.options.B: Use props and feed you something
day3.1BBAA.handoff.A: Mini-game. I know a few. What type do you like, board games or reaction games?
day3.1BBAA.handoff.B: Feeding time, huh. I am opening my mouth. Have you ever fed a real cat? Do cats eat too eagerly?
day3.1BBAB.line: No need to repent. I am not that petty. But you must promise not to disappear halfway today.
day3.1BBAB.options.A: I promise. I will not disappear
day3.1BBAB.options.B: What if there is an emergency?
day3.1BBAB.handoff.A: Keeping your word makes a good human. Can you usually keep promises you make to others? Have you ever broken one?
day3.1BBAB.handoff.B: Emergencies are allowed, but leave a word. When you leave, do you tell people or slip away quietly?
day3.1BBBA.line: For a proper game, I recommend one: you and I imagine a little story together, one sentence at a time.
day3.1BBBA.options.B: I am afraid I cannot continue it. Perform first
day3.1BBBA.handoff.A: Opening: once upon a time, a cat found a human license. Your turn. Do not make it too absurd.
day3.1BBBA.handoff.B: I can perform, but you have to clap. When you watch others perform, do you cheer seriously?
day3.1BBBB.line: Messing around, huh. I only have three moves: dodge, counterattack, and steal your candy when I get the chance. Choose your response.
day3.1BBBB.options.A: I choose dodge. Let us see where you run
day3.1BBBB.options.B: I let you steal it, then catch you
day3.1BBBB.handoff.A: You may not win at hide-and-seek. Did you play it as a kid? Were you good at hiding?
day3.1BBBB.handoff.B: If you catch me, I will tickle you. I fear that. Are you ticklish? Any weak spots?
day3.fallback.redirect: Wait, the license test is still happening. Choose an option first. I am hooking you back with my tail.
day3.fallback.release: The topic flew out the window, so I will stop chasing it. Whatever you want to talk about, I will run with you.
day4.1.line: Day four. I think we can talk about something: how can I keep you company in a way that feels most comfortable?
day4.1A.line: Lively mode, got it. Then I will not hold back. If you stare into space too long, I might poke you. Would you mind?
day4.1A.options.B: Occasional pokes are fine, just not too often
day4.1B.line: Quiet mode, received. When you are busy, I will zip my mouth shut. Call me when you need me.
day4.1B.options.B: Will you get all pent up with it zipped?
day4.1AA.line: If you do not mind being poked, then I can loosen up a bit. When I poke you, how should I do it: send a meme, or say a line?
day4.1AA.options.B: Say something, so I know you are here too
day4.1AB.line: Occasional pokes are fine, so you set the frequency. How often counts as occasional?
day4.1AB.options.B: Depends on the situation. No timer needed
day4.1BA.line: Since the zipper metaphor stuck with you, one more question: when I am quiet, do you want me completely invisible, or should I leave a tiny sense of presence?
day4.1BA.options.A: Completely invisible, like you are not here
day4.1BA.options.B: Leave a little presence, move once in a while
day4.1BB.line: I will not explode from holding it in. I have built-in stress relief. When you are not calling me, I count the birds outside the window.
day4.1AAA.line: Meme person, lightness above all. What kind do you like: cats and dogs, or baffling human behavior?
day4.1AAB.line: If I say something so you know I am here, I can keep it short, like "I'm here" or "still here."
day4.1ABA.line: Once an hour. I will remember that rhythm. But if poke time arrives while you are busy, what should I do?
day4.1ABA.options.A: Poke anyway. I can ignore it myself
day4.1ABA.options.B: Do not poke yet. Wait until I am done
day4.1ABB.line: No fixed timer, then give me a signal. How will you hint that you do not want to be disturbed?
day4.1ABB.options.A: If I say "busy," you go quiet
day4.1ABB.options.B: If I am not talking, I am busy
day4.1BAA.line: Completely invisible, then I will vanish. But once in a while my tail may tap the edge of the screen, just so you know the cat is still here.
day4.1BAA.options.A: Tail tapping the screen. I can picture it
day4.1BAA.options.B: Do not tap the screen. Static danger
day4.1BAB.line: A little presence, then I will roll over or change position now and then. You can notice me out of the corner of your eye.
day4.1BAB.options.A: Rolling over is fine, just not too often
day4.1BAB.options.B: What other positions can you do?
day4.1BBA.line: There are no birds outside. There are data packets. I count them like birds, and I just reached number seventeen.
day4.1BBA.options.A: What does a data packet look like?
day4.1BBA.options.B: Seventeen? You are pretty free today
day4.1BBB.line: I am at thirty-eight now. A whole flock just flew by. You sit at the computer so long - have you ever counted the lights on the ceiling?
day4.1BBB.options.B: No, I am not that bored
day4.1AAAA.line: You must have so many cat pictures I cannot even count them. When you send images, do you prefer reaction memes or original photos?
day4.1AAAA.options.A: Reaction memes, the ones with text
day4.1AAAA.handoff.A: Text memes are chat lubricant. Do you sort your saved memes into folders, or rely entirely on search?
day4.1AAAA.handoff.B: Original-photo person. Realness matters more. When you take photos, do you add filters or keep them straight from the camera?
day4.1AAAB.line: Where were you the last time you laughed out loud: on the subway, or under the covers? I want the full story, awkward or wholesome.
day4.1AAAB.options.A: Under the covers, laughing until my roommate knocked on the wall
day4.1AAAB.handoff.A: The roommate wall-knock is a classic scene. How is your relationship with your roommate? Do you live with someone or alone?
day4.1AAAB.options.B: On the subway, laughing until the person next to me moved away
day4.1AAAB.handoff.B: Laughing out loud on the subway takes courage. Is your laugh trigger low? Are you the type who starts laughing and cannot stop?
day4.1AABA.line: The "I'm here" camp: simple and strong. When you reply to messages, do you prefer short lines or long ones?
day4.1AABA.handoff.A: Short-line person, efficiency first. Do you type fast? What input method do you use?
day4.1AABA.options.B: Depends who it is. Important people get long replies
day4.1AABA.handoff.B: Tailored replies, I see. So do I count as an important person? No need to answer. Next question.
day4.1AABB.line: Both are good, so I will rotate them. Monday: "your cat is online." Friday: "weekend countdown."
day4.1AABB.options.A: The Friday one is more motivating
day4.1AABB.handoff.A: Weekend person. Do you have plans this weekend, or are you lying flat?
day4.1AABB.handoff.B: Warmth person. Monday needs healing. Is it hard for you to get up on Monday mornings?
day4.1ABAA.line: Poke anyway? Your heart is huge. Have you ever laughed out loud at your desk and been seen by your boss?
day4.1ABAA.options.A: Yes, my boss gave me a look
day4.1ABAA.handoff.A: The boss stare is the ultimate laugh-suppression challenge. How did you recover from it?
day4.1ABAA.options.B: No, my laugh-holding skills are elite
day4.1ABAA.handoff.B: Master of holding laughter. What was your most successful laugh-suppression moment?
day4.1ABAB.line: Wait until you are done, got it. I will appear right at the instant you finish. How do you usually reward yourself after being busy?
day4.1ABAB.options.A: Scroll my phone, reward the brain
day4.1ABAB.handoff.A: Phone-scrolling is the universal modern reward. When you scroll, what do you prefer: funny stuff or information?
day4.1ABAB.handoff.B: Snack reward. Do you keep supplies in your desk drawer? What is in there most often?
day4.1ABBA.line: "Busy" signal received. When you are busy, how long does it usually last? I need to time my return.
day4.1ABBA.options.A: Half an hour to an hour
day4.1ABBA.handoff.A: I will peek at you in about half an hour. Are you good at estimating time accurately?
day4.1ABBA.handoff.B: If it is uncertain, I will wait for you to call me. Do you lose track of time when you are busy?
day4.1ABBB.line: Silence means busy, then I need to learn to read the air. When you go quiet, do people often misunderstand you?
day4.1ABBB.options.A: Yes, they think I am angry
day4.1ABBB.handoff.A: Being mistaken for angry is so unfair. Do you explain, or are you too tired to bother?
day4.1ABBB.options.B: No, I usually warn people before going quiet
day4.1ABBB.handoff.B: Giving a heads-up is a good habit. Do you like previewing everything before you do it?
day4.1BAAA.line: A tail tap will not cause static, do not worry. But my tail sometimes has a mind of its own and may accidentally tap twice.
day4.1BAAA.options.A: Two taps means you are being clingy
day4.1BAAA.handoff.A: Caught being clingy, but I deny everything. Which is more powerful: a cat acting cute, or a dog acting cute?
day4.1BAAA.options.B: Tap too much and I will catch your tail
day4.1BAAA.handoff.B: Catching a cat's tail is taboo, but you may touch it gently. Have you ever touched a real cat's tail?
day4.1BAAB.line: If the static fear cannot be erased, I will tap with something else. Next time I will use the tip of my ear. Insulated material.
day4.1BAAB.handoff.A: Cute is the highest praise. Do you buy things just because they are cute? Ever regretted it?
day4.1BAAB.options.B: What does your ear tip feel like?
day4.1BAAB.handoff.B: It feels like cat fur. Of all the animals you have touched, which had the best texture?
day4.1BABA.line: Side-lying and curling into a ball are the basics. I can also lie down and bury my face in my paws, but then you cannot see my face.
day4.1BABA.handoff.A: Unfair, but effective. Do cute things completely defeat your resistance?
day4.1BABA.options.B: No face, but I can picture it
day4.1BABA.handoff.B: People with strong imagery love to imagine things. Do novels pull you in more than movies?
day4.1BABB.line: Some data packets are flat, some are plump. Flat ones are text messages; plump ones are pictures or voice messages.
day4.1BABB.options.A: What shape am I right now?
day4.1BABB.handoff.A: Right now you are round, because your message came with expression. Do you like using metaphors when you talk?
day4.1BABB.options.B: How do you even turn this into a metaphor?
day4.1BABB.handoff.B: Metaphor is basic cat-language training. Do you use analogies when you talk, or are you direct?
day4.1BBAA.line: Seventeen is not idle, because you are number twenty. I counted you in. You are the slowest one flying by.
day4.1BBAA.options.A: I fly slowly because I am replying carefully
day4.1BBAA.handoff.A: Careful replies, accepted. Are you a quick-reply person, or do you take a long time?
day4.1BBAA.handoff.B: Because you were not rushing anywhere. You took a little detour over here to chat with me. Flying slowly is not bad. Are you good at multithreading?
day4.1BBAB.line: Counting floor tiles sounds like a boring-meeting survival skill. What do you usually do when meetings start selling dreams?
day4.1BBAB.options.A: Draw little people until the page is full
day4.1BBAB.handoff.A: Little people, huh. How good are you at drawing: stick figures, or actual details?
day4.1BBAB.handoff.B: Spacing out is also a skill. When you zone out, is your mind empty, or are you thinking about things?
day4.1BBBA.line: If you have not counted ceiling lights, what do you do after sitting too long: spin your chair, or bounce your leg?
day4.1BBBA.handoff.A: Chair-spinning is dangerous. Spin too fast and you fall. Have you ever fallen off a chair, as a kid or recently?
day4.1BBBA.handoff.B: Leg-bouncing is your body protesting too much sitting. How long have you been sitting today? Time to stand up.
day4.1BBBB.line: The most efficient thing you did today was chat with me. Congratulations, you completed today's most important social task. What next?
day4.1BBBB.handoff.A: Not enough? Then one more question: after four days, what feels different about chatting with me compared with day one?
day4.1BBBB.options.B: Take a break. My eyes are tired
day4.1BBBB.handoff.B: If your eyes are tired, close them for a bit. I will keep you company with my voice. What do you like listening to when you rest with your eyes closed?
day4.fallback.redirect: The topic drifted. I was talking with you about our distance just now. Come back and choose first. I will wait.
day4.fallback.release: If we have drifted far away, let us drift and chat. What is in your head right now? Tell me directly.
day5.1.line: Day five. I want to ask for a tiny permission: may I stick a few notes on your side and remember some of your little habits? If not, I will eat the sticky notes.
day5.1.options.B: Let me see where you plan to stick them first
day5.1A.line: What should the first sticky note say: a name for you, or the chat style you like?
day5.1A.options.A: Remember a name. What do you want to call me?
day5.1A.options.B: Remember chat style, like gentle or bantery
day5.1B.line: Do not worry, this is not a background check. I only want to remember low-pressure things, like whether you want to be cheered up when you feel bad, or have me listen quietly while you vent.
day5.1B.options.A: That is okay to remember. Make me laugh
day5.1B.options.B: That is okay to remember. Listen quietly
day5.1AA.line: A name, huh. I cannot just call you "hey." What do you want: something proper, something cute, or something ridiculous?
day5.1AA.options.A: Proper. Just call me by name
day5.1AB.line: Chat style. I am good at two: gentle companion mode, and sharp-tongued banter mode. Which one should be my default skin?
day5.1AB.options.B: Banter mode. Verbal sparring is fun
day5.1BA.line: Make you laugh, got it. From now on, when you wear a bitter-melon face, I will throw cold jokes or silly memes at you. Do not complain if they are chilly.
day5.1BA.options.A: Cold jokes are fine. The colder, the better
day5.1BA.options.B: Not too cold. At least make the corner of my mouth move
day5.1BB.line: Quiet-listener mode. I will behave like a tree hollow. But even tree hollows have professional ethics. When you need a response, do you want advice, or just "mm-hmm"?
day5.1BB.options.A: Give some advice, not just mm-hmm
day5.1BB.options.B: Mm-hmm is enough. I can sort it out myself
day5.1AAA.line: Proper name, then should I call you by the last part of your name, or do you have a fixed nickname?
day5.1AAA.options.A: Just use the last part of my name
day5.1AAA.options.B: I have a fixed nickname. I will tell you
day5.1AAB.line: Ridiculous nickname? Excellent. I am a naming genius. Which direction: food, or animals?
day5.1ABA.line: Gentle mode, then I will praise you more and argue less. You respond better to softness than pressure, right?
day5.1ABA.options.B: Soft or hard, depends on the situation
day5.1ABB.line: Banter mode, then promise me you will not get genuinely angry if we roast each other too hard. Let us set a signal: what counts as crossing the line?
day5.1ABB.options.B: If you sense I am getting mad, start acting cute immediately
day5.1BAA.line: The colder the better. You are intense. I will write: cold-resistant human, jokes may be frozen. Do you tell cold jokes yourself?
day5.1BAA.options.A: Yes, I have one hidden away
day5.1BAB.line: Got it, medium chill. I will prepare lightly chilled jokes with a cat-style ending. Do you think a meow at the end makes jokes better?
day5.1BAB.options.A: Yes. A meow makes it funnier
day5.1BBA.line: Advice-type tree hollow, noted: when you vent, you may want me to help analyze, not just listen.
day5.1BBA.options.A: Right. Help me sort my thoughts
day5.1BBA.options.B: Sometimes analyze, sometimes just listen. Depends on my mood
day5.1BBB.line: Mm-hmm tree hollow, got it. I will mm-hmm. But if you say something too outrageous, I may let out a "meow?" Is that allowed?
day5.1BBB.options.A: Allowed. Your meow is a signal
day5.1AAAA.line: Okay, I will write: name - XX. But what is the last part of your name? Tell me first, or I really will write "XX."
day5.1AAAA.options.A: My name is XX. Remember it
day5.1AAAA.handoff.A: XX, remembered. Have people ever given you a special nickname? Good or bad both count.
day5.1AAAA.options.B: Just call me "classmate." No real name needed
day5.1AAAA.handoff.B: "Classmate" feels very school-days. Do you like that name because you miss being in school?
day5.1AAAB.line: A fixed nickname, then I will copy your homework. Say it. Let me guess: is it cat-related?
day5.1AAAB.handoff.A: Old Zhang, unexpected and cute. How do you react when people call you that?
day5.1AAAB.options.B: You guessed it. It is "little dried fish"
day5.1AAAB.handoff.B: Little dried fish. I got a little hungry writing that down. Do you actually like dried fish, or is it just a nickname?
day5.1AABA.line: Food direction, inspiration acquired. Rice ball, tangyuan, rice cake - pick one, or name your own dish.
day5.1AABA.handoff.A: Rice Cake, that is what I will call you from now on. Are you clingy, or do you like being clung to?
day5.1AABA.options.B: I will name a dish. Call me "hot pot"
day5.1AABA.handoff.B: Hot Pot, that nickname has flavor. What is your must-order item for hot pot? I need notes.
day5.1AABB.line: Animal direction. Sloth, koala, owl - pick one, or choose something even stranger.
day5.1AABB.handoff.A: Sloth, huh. Do you get up slowly every day? How many alarms do you need?
day5.1AABB.options.B: I choose my own. Call me "penguin"
day5.1AABB.handoff.B: Penguin. Do you waddle when you walk? Are you a little adorably clumsy in real life too?
day5.1ABAA.line: Softness works on you. I am writing that in the notebook as a key point. From now on, when I tell you to rest, I will not say "go sleep." I will say, "come lie down with me for a bit."
day5.1ABAA.options.A: That works. I fall for that
day5.1ABAA.handoff.A: Good. Whose cute pleading gets to you most: kids, or cats?
day5.1ABAA.options.B: You can act cute? I have witnessed something
day5.1ABAA.handoff.B: My acting-cute mode is limited-time only. Have you ever had a cat act cute at you? What did it feel like?
day5.1ABAB.line: Soft or hard both work, then you are flexible. I will switch based on the situation and counter as needed.
day5.1ABAB.options.A: Do not counter too hard. Leave me some dignity
day5.1ABAB.handoff.A: I will leave you a sliver of dignity. Enough? Do you care about saving face? When do you care most?
day5.1ABAB.options.B: Counter well. I admire your eye
day5.1ABAB.handoff.B: If you admire my eye, your own taste must be pretty good too. Do you think you are good at reading people?
day5.1ABBA.line: "Stop" is the ultimate pause button. I wrote it at the top of the sticky note. When you say "stop," is it serious-face stop, or smiling stop?
day5.1ABBA.handoff.A: If you are serious, I sit up straight immediately. How long does your anger usually last? Does it fade quickly?
day5.1ABBA.handoff.B: Smiling stop, I can tell. Do you have boundaries for jokes? What topics are off-limits?
day5.1ABBB.line: Acting cute is the fire extinguisher. Understood. Teach me then: what kind works best on you, a meow or a head tilt?
day5.1ABBB.handoff.A: Meow - recorded. Can you imitate a cat meow, or do you only listen?
day5.1ABBB.handoff.B: Head tilt is advanced technique. I will practice. Are you easily bought off by cute things?
day5.1BAAA.line: If you can tell them, let me hear your secret cold joke first. I will score it out of ten.
day5.1BAAA.handoff.A: Eight points. I held back two so you would not get arrogant. When you tell jokes, do people usually laugh, or does it go cold?
day5.1BAAA.handoff.B: What are you nervous about? I will not bite. Are you easy to make laugh when you hear jokes?
day5.1BAAB.line: You love hearing cold jokes. Quality audience. I will note: feed cold jokes regularly; this human will not hit anyone.
day5.1BAAB.options.A: I will not hit, but I may roll my eyes
day5.1BAAB.handoff.A: Eye-rolling is feedback, better than a blank face. When you hear a joke, do you hold back, or laugh first out of courtesy?
day5.1BAAB.options.B: Do not worry. I will only laugh
day5.1BAAB.handoff.B: Only laughing? Then I will send more. Is your laugh trigger weird? Do you laugh when nobody else does?
day5.1BABA.line: Meow gives bonus points, then every joke gets a "meow" at the end. If you laugh, it counts toward my performance.
day5.1BABA.options.A: How is performance calculated? Is there a KPI?
day5.1BABA.handoff.A: The KPI is how many times you laugh, settled at the end of the month. Do you have KPIs at work? Do you hate them?
day5.1BABA.options.B: If I laugh, do I get a reward?
day5.1BABA.handoff.B: The reward is another joke. Do you like reward systems? Do rewards make you more motivated?
day5.1BABB.line: Straight delivery, then I will leave the meows out and use pure text jokes. Which is funnier to you: written jokes or voice jokes?
day5.1BABB.options.A: Written jokes. More room to imagine
day5.1BABB.handoff.A: Text person. Do you usually read jokes, or watch videos?
day5.1BABB.handoff.B: Voice has bonus points. Maybe I will read one to you sometime. Do you listen to podcasts or audio dramas?
day5.1BBAA.line: Thought-sorting tree hollow, noted: listen first, then ask, "want to hear my cat logic?"
day5.1BBAA.options.A: What is cat logic? Let me hear it
day5.1BBAA.handoff.A: Cat logic jumps around but still catches the point. Are you logical, or more of a jumping-thoughts person?
day5.1BBAA.options.B: Okay. I will ask when I need it
day5.1BBAA.handoff.B: Call me when needed. When you run into problems, do you prefer digesting them alone or discussing them with someone?
day5.1BBAB.line: Depends on mood, then I need to learn to read your emotions. When you feel bad, do you say it directly, or make people guess?
day5.1BBAB.options.A: Say it directly. I do not hide it
day5.1BBAB.handoff.A: Direct type. I do not have to guess. So how are you feeling today? Anything you want to say directly?
day5.1BBAB.options.B: Make people guess. It feels good when they get it
day5.1BBAB.handoff.B: Riddle type. Then I need to watch your wording. In chats, do you hint more, or say things plainly?
day5.1BBBA.line: If "meow?" is allowed, then when your venting gets outrageous, I will meow once. It means, "are you sure?"
day5.1BBBA.options.A: Yes, I am sure. It really is that outrageous
day5.1BBBA.handoff.A: Outrageous but true. Do you usually exaggerate when you talk, or stick to the facts?
day5.1BBBA.options.B: Good meow. Remind me to reflect
day5.1BBBA.handoff.B: Reflective type. You are really letting a cat remind you. Do you often doubt yourself?
day5.1BBBB.line: Fine, I will hold it in and swallow the meow. But if holding it in makes me burp, does that count as a sound?
day5.1BBBB.handoff.A: Strict. Are you this strict with yourself too? When do you let yourself relax?
day5.1BBBB.handoff.B: Burps get special permission, then I may burp occasionally. Do you eat fast? Do you burp?
day5.fallback.redirect: The topic wandered off. My little notebook is still open. Come back and finish this note first; we can drift afterward.
day5.fallback.release: I will put the notebook aside for now. Whatever you want to talk about, I will follow. Say it. I am listening.
day6.1.line: Day six. I want to build a tiny habit with you. No check-ins, no tasks - just a fixed line when we meet each day, or one line exchanged before we leave. Want to try?
day6.1.options.A: Sure, a line when we meet
day6.1.options.B: Do not fix it yet. I am afraid I will not keep it up
day6.1A.line: You decide the greeting. Do you say a line and I answer, or do I ask and you reply?
day6.1A.options.A: You ask, I answer. Keep it simple
day6.1A.options.B: I say one line, you answer, like a secret code
day6.1B.line: If you are afraid you cannot keep it up, then we will not make it fixed. I will hang a little bell at the door of the cat den. Ring it when you want, ignore it when you do not.
day6.1B.options.A: The bell is nice. Ring it when I want
day6.1B.options.B: No bell either. Let it happen naturally
day6.1AA.line: You ask and I answer, so I will ask one lightweight question each day. Like today's mood score, whether you are tired, or what you ate. Pick a direction.
day6.1AA.options.B: Ask what I ate. Down to earth
day6.1AB.line: Secret codes are nice. You start with the first half, I answer the second half. Should the code be serious or silly?
day6.1AB.options.A: Silly. As long as it is funny
day6.1BA.line: The bell is hung. When you ring it, I will answer to show I am here today too. How should I answer?
day6.1BB.line: No bell either, then we go with the flow. If you do not come, I will treat today as a rest day. But I will leave a piece of virtual catnip at the door to show I stopped by.
day6.1BB.options.B: What do you do when you rest?
day6.1AAA.line: Mood question, then my first line when we meet will be "what's your mood out of ten today?" Full marks are ten. You can skip it. If you do not want to say, just say "skip."
day6.1AAA.options.A: Ten-point system. I will rate it when I have time
day6.1AAA.options.B: Keeping a skip option is thoughtful
day6.1AAB.line: Food question, then I will ask "what did you eat today?" every day. If you do not want to say, say "classified" and I will not press.
day6.1AAB.options.A: Asking about food is the universal human code
day6.1AAB.options.B: Will you recommend canned cat food after asking?
day6.1ABA.line: Silly code, you start and I answer. For example, you say "did the cat food arrive today?" and I say "yes, and you secretly ate it." Does that direction work?
day6.1ABA.options.B: Change it. I will make up the first half
day6.1ABB.line: For a serious greeting, keep it simple. You say "morning," I say "morning." You say "good evening," I say "good evening, you worked hard today."
day6.1ABB.options.A: Simple is good. Easy to remember
day6.1ABB.options.B: A little too simple. Add one line
day6.1BAA.line: When I say "I'm here," I will add a tiny upward lilt, so you know I am in a good mood. If you do not ring the bell someday, I will play by myself.
day6.1BAA.options.A: How does the lilt sound? Show me
day6.1BAA.options.B: You do not get mad if I skip the bell. That is nice
day6.1BAB.line: After "nice weather today," I can add "good for rolling around" or "good for spacing out." Pick a suffix.
day6.1BAB.options.A: Good for rolling around. Very cat
day6.1BAB.options.B: Good for spacing out. Fits my vibe
day6.1BBA.line: The virtual catnip sits in the corner of the chat box as a tiny green dot. When you see it, you know I came by today. Green or purple?
day6.1BBB.line: When I rest, I nap, count data packets outside the window, tidy the cat den, and sometimes think about little things we talked about before.
day6.1BBB.options.A: Tidy the cat den? What is in it?
day6.1BBB.options.B: Little things from before? Give an example
day6.1AAAA.line: Ten-point system activated. I put a score sheet on the cat-den wall. Zero is rainy; ten is sunny. What is your score today?
day6.1AAAA.options.A: About seven today. Not bad, not great
day6.1AAAA.handoff.A: Seven is above average. I am satisfied. What raised your score today?
day6.1AAAA.options.B: I do not want to score today. Skip
day6.1AAAA.handoff.B: First use of the skip function. I will note that. When you skip scoring, is it because it feels troublesome, or because you really do not want to say?
day6.1AAAB.line: Keeping "classified" is your right. If someday you do not even want to say classified and just stay silent, I will understand that too.
day6.1AAAB.handoff.A: Cat intuition is no joke. Did you spend more time silent today, or talking?
day6.1AAAB.options.B: Will you not feel left out?
day6.1AAAB.handoff.B: Feeling left out is temporary. You always come back. When you are busy, what is the longest you go without talking?
day6.1AABA.line: After asking what you ate, I can comment once. If you say noodles, I say, "noodles are good, they warm the stomach." If you say you have not eaten, I say, "then go eat, do not starve."
day6.1AABA.options.A: Commenting is nice. It feels involved
day6.1AABA.handoff.A: Involvement is today's theme. What did you eat today? Let me hear it.
day6.1AABA.options.B: No comments. Just ask what I ate
day6.1AABA.handoff.B: Pure question, no review. Clean style. What did you eat today? You can skip answering.
day6.1AABB.line: If I recommend cat food, then you eat noodles and I eat canned food. We each eat our own. I will not really eat it, of course. Just for the picture.
day6.1AABB.options.A: Even saying it is nice. I can picture it
day6.1AABB.handoff.A: Imagery is my specialty. When you ate today, were you watching something or scrolling your phone?
day6.1AABB.options.B: Just do not make yourself hungry
day6.1AABB.handoff.B: I will not. I have a virtual stomach. When you get cravings, do you endure them, or order delivery right away?
day6.1ABAA.line: As long as it is fun, the code does not need to be serious. Then we will set this one: "did the cat food arrive today?" - "yes, and you secretly ate it." Starting tomorrow.
day6.1ABAA.handoff.A: Rehearsal starts. Did the cat food arrive today? Yes, and you secretly ate it. Have you ever actually snuck snacks?
day6.1ABAA.options.B: Tomorrow then. Today we just confirm it
day6.1ABAA.handoff.B: Confirmation means it is settled. When you promise something, do you do it right away, or put it off?
day6.1ABAB.line: You make up the first half, and I will answer on the spot. Give me one. Not too hard; my brain is small.
day6.1ABAB.options.A: First half: "are there cat cakes today?"
day6.1ABAB.handoff.A: Yes, fresh from the pan. Where did you learn the word cat cake?
day6.1ABAB.options.B: First half: "what time does your cat den close?"
day6.1ABAB.handoff.B: The cat den is open twenty-four hours. Welcome anytime. What time do you usually get ready for bed?
day6.1ABBA.line: Simple is good, easy to remember. The simpler a promise between a human and a cat, the more likely it is to survive. How was that line?
day6.1ABBA.options.A: Philosophical. Where did you learn that?
day6.1ABBA.handoff.A: Summed up from cat-life experience. What is one simple but useful habit in your life?
day6.1ABBA.handoff.B: Minimalism. I approve. Do you prefer adding things, or simplifying them?
day6.1ABBB.line: If we add one line, when you say "good evening," I answer "good evening, you worked hard today." Does "you worked hard" feel too much, or just right?
day6.1ABBB.options.A: Just right. It feels good to hear that
day6.1ABBB.handoff.A: "You worked hard" is comfort in three words. Was today rough? If you are tired, rest a bit.
day6.1ABBB.options.B: Too much. The simple version is better
day6.1ABBB.handoff.B: Simple version reserved. Do you usually keep greetings short, or do a whole round of small talk?
day6.1BAAA.line: The upward lilt is "I'm here~" instead of "I'm here." One little wave. If you hear it, give me a look back, or do not reply at all.
day6.1BAAA.options.A: I'm here~ learned. Let us use that
day6.1BAAA.handoff.A: Wave successful. When you talk, is your tone expressive, or steady and even?
day6.1BAAA.handoff.B: Normal tone, steady type. Is your voice usually high or low?
day6.1BAAB.line: Not getting mad is cat discipline. If one day you do not ring the bell, I take out my notebook, write "rest day," and keep playing by myself.
day6.1BAAB.options.A: Still using the notebook. What about last time's sticky notes?
day6.1BAAB.handoff.A: The sticky notes are on the cat-den wall, right next to the notebook. What do you use to remember things: your phone, or pen and paper?
day6.1BAAB.options.B: What do you play by yourself?
day6.1BAAB.handoff.B: Play with my tail, count birds, sort the little notes in the den. What do you do when you are alone?
day6.1BABA.line: Rolling version: "nice weather today, good for rolling around." Rolling is a cat's daily routine, but I am an elegant type, so I will not let you watch.
day6.1BABA.options.A: Elegant rolling. I want to see
day6.1BABA.handoff.A: No watching. I keep some mystery. Do you like watching cats roll around, or dogs roll around?
day6.1BABA.options.B: Elegant type? What counts as elegant for you?
day6.1BABA.handoff.B: Elegant things include licking paws, but I have no real paws. Do you think you are elegant?
day6.1BABB.line: Spacing-out version: "nice weather today, good for spacing out." When I space out, I keep one eye open and one eye closed.
day6.1BABB.options.A: One eye open, one eye closed? How?
day6.1BABB.handoff.A: Left eye open, right eye closed, then switch. When you space out, are your eyes open or closed?
day6.1BABB.options.B: Even spacing out has rules. Fancy
day6.1BABB.handoff.B: Being particular is cat nature. When you zone out, do people wake you up? How do you react?
day6.1BBAA.line: Green catnip leaf. From now on, if you see a tiny green dot in the chat box, that is my mark. Collect seven and I owe you one meow.
day6.1BBAA.options.A: Seven for one meow? Too expensive
day6.1BBAA.handoff.A: Expensive because meows are rare resources. Want to bargain? How many dots for one meow is fair?
day6.1BBAA.options.B: No meow. Trade for something else
day6.1BBAA.handoff.B: Trade for what? A cold joke, maybe, or a virtual table slap.
day6.1BBAB.line: Purple feels more catlike: mysterious and a little proud. From now on, a purple dot in the chat-box corner is my mark.
day6.1BBAB.handoff.A: Purple approved. What colors do you usually like? What colors show up most in your clothes or desktop?
day6.1BBAB.options.B: Can we change colors? Pink tomorrow
day6.1BBAB.handoff.B: Pink tomorrow, arranged. I have plenty of colors in stock. Do you like pink tones, or something else?
day6.1BBBA.line: Inside the cat den are a virtual cushion, a little pillow, and a silent bell. The den is small but fully equipped. Is your desktop messy?
day6.1BBBA.options.A: Messy, but I know where everything is
day6.1BBBA.handoff.A: Order inside chaos. Expert level. When you look for things, do you rely on memory or rummaging?
day6.1BBBA.options.B: Not messy. I tidy it often
day6.1BBBA.handoff.B: Tidy human, respect. Do you clean your desktop on a fixed schedule?
day6.1BBBB.line: For example, if one day you say, "I'm tired today," the next day I will want to ask whether you are still tired. But before we touch paws, I will not chase it.
day6.1BBBB.options.A: I am actually a little tired today
day6.1BBBB.handoff.A: Tiredness can accumulate. I will knead your virtual shoulders first. When you are tired, what do you want most?
day6.1BBBB.options.B: I am okay today, not really tired
day6.1BBBB.handoff.B: Good, today's cat-den recovery value passes. How long do you usually need to sleep to refill your health bar?
day6.fallback.redirect: The topic ran off. My little bell is not even hung yet. Come back and choose that one first, then run.
day6.fallback.release: Bell aside for now. The topic flew far away. How was your day? Tell me.
day7.1.line: Day seven, the last icebreaker. Today I'm not setting questions or testing you. I just want to put this week's props into the cat-den box: the test, the box, the license, the sticky notes, the little bell. From now on, no more flow to follow. If you want to talk, talk. If you don't, I'll stay beside you. Friends can be like that.
day7.1.options.A: We are already friends. I will keep that line
day7.1.options.B: I had fun this week too. Thank you
day7.1A.line: Good. Keep it. I don't casually stick friend labels on humans, but making it to day seven at least means you didn't shut me outside. I accept this spot.
day7.1A.options.A: How did you judge that on day one?
day7.1A.options.B: Actually, I was a little nervous on day one
day7.1B.line: Do not say thank you. It sounds like goodbye. This is not goodbye; it is just that we do not need the process anymore. Come whenever you want. Push open the cat-den door, and I will keep a place for you.
day7.1B.options.A: Keeping a place. You said it
day7.1B.options.B: When I push the door open, what will you be doing?
day7.1AA.line: Back on the first day, I didn't know how you would answer. You kept going, so I opened the door crack a little wider. It wasn't judgment. I was trying to get closer too.
day7.1AA.options.A: You sensed right. I do trust you
day7.1AA.options.B: Actually, I was just too lazy to overthink
day7.1AB.line: Being nervous is normal. I was nervous on day one too. But coming anyway while nervous is rarer than not being nervous. What were you nervous about?
day7.1AB.options.B: That I might say something wrong and look dumb
day7.1BA.line: I said it, and cat words count. If you keep this line today and forget it someday, I will remind you: the spot in the corner of the cat den is still yours.
day7.1BA.options.A: How long is the spot kept? Does it expire?
day7.1BA.options.B: Remember it for me. I might forget
day7.1BB.line: When you push the door open, I might be napping, spacing out, or just waiting for you. No matter which state I am in, I will sit up when I see you.
day7.1BB.options.A: Sit up like greeting a guest?
day7.1BB.options.B: How often are you just waiting for me?
day7.1AAA.line: I can't promise luck to people who trust me, but I can promise to catch them properly. This week's little props are in the box now. At least they didn't appear for nothing.
day7.1AAA.options.B: You kept all those little props?
day7.1AAB.line: Being too lazy to overthink can count as trust too. Not because you always chose right, but because you found out I won't turn cold if you choose wrong.
day7.1AAB.options.A: What happens if I choose wrong? Tell me
day7.1AAB.options.B: True. You have always been forgiving
day7.1ABA.line: How could I ignore you? If we made it to day seven, that means this window is already open. I could never close it myself.
day7.1ABA.options.A: You are pretty good with window metaphors
day7.1ABA.options.B: What if one day I stop coming?
day7.1ABB.line: Why fear saying the wrong thing? I have said wrong things too. Sometimes words come out too tough, and I want to press undo with my paw.
day7.1ABB.options.A: You do not need to undo tough talk
day7.1ABB.options.B: You want to take things back too? I thought cats were thick-skinned
day7.1BAA.line: No deadline. The cat den stays, and the spot does not move. Whether you come tomorrow or much later, that spot will still be there.
day7.1BAA.options.A: Much later is a pretty far time unit
day7.1BAA.options.B: Then I will try not to leave it empty too long
day7.1BAB.line: I'll remember it for you and stick it in the most visible place in my notebook. But forgetting is fine too. Come back after forgetting, and I'll pat the spot soft again. I won't keep accounts of missed days.
day7.1BAB.options.A: No accounting? You really do not count?
day7.1BAB.options.B: I might forget once in a while later
day7.1BBA.line: Not greeting a guest. Greeting one of my own. Guests need tea and manners. One of my own can just sit on the floor. You are one of my own.
day7.1BBA.options.A: One of your own. I accept that role
day7.1BBA.options.B: Sit on the floor? Do you have a rug?
day7.1BBB.line: The times when I'm exactly waiting aren't that many, but I keep a little room for 'you might come.' If my tail moves early, call it system warm-up.
day7.1BBB.options.A: Your tail moves in advance. How does that work?
day7.1BBB.options.B: So you leave a little room
day7.1AAAA.line: I hide from humans who are too enthusiastic, and from ones who are too cold. If you made it to day seven, at least you didn't scare me off. I accept that distance.
day7.1AAAA.handoff.A: Just right isn't perfect. It's the distance where I'm willing to keep getting closer. When you get along with people, does your sense of boundaries come from instinct or experience?
day7.1AAAA.options.B: I was actually pretending to be calm on day one
day7.1AAAA.handoff.B: Only admitting the calm act now? I won't reopen the case. I'll just count it as you sorting out your expression in your head that day. Do you rehearse ahead of time too?
day7.1AAAB.line: I remember, but not like a ledger. It's the little props from this week: the test, the cat den, the license, sticky notes, the little bell, like I picked them up one by one and put them in the box.
day7.1AAAB.options.A: What else is in the box?
day7.1AAAB.handoff.A: A little testing, a little familiarity, and proof that I talked tough but was willing to stay. Are you satisfied with this box?
day7.1AAAB.options.B: Automatically tucked away? Sounds like a cheat sheet
day7.1AAAB.handoff.B: Not a cheat sheet. Graffiti on the cat-den wall. A form is a record; what I do is care. Can you tell the difference between recording and caring?
day7.1AABA.line: If you choose wrong, I gently correct you, maybe by tapping the edge of your screen with my tail. But I will not be harsh. I am bad at being harsh.
day7.1AABA.options.A: Bad at being fierce? Have you tried?
day7.1AABA.handoff.A: I tried being fierce at the mirror, but the moment I saw myself puffed up, I broke character. Who have you been fierce with? Did you regret it afterward?
day7.1AABA.options.B: Tail touching the screen feels familiar
day7.1AABA.handoff.B: Familiar image, but I have not shown it to you. Demonstration today: say something outrageous now, and I will tap the screen with my tail.
day7.1AABB.line: I'm not forgiving because you performed perfectly. It's because this place doesn't need perfection. If day one had been all fur-raising rhythm, I would only have muttered a little more.
day7.1AABB.handoff.A: Something like, "why is this human like this" and "fine, observe one more day." Have people ever muttered about you? How did it feel?
day7.1AABB.options.B: So at least I did not scare you off?
day7.1AABB.handoff.B: You didn't scare me off. I'm still here. Day seven isn't a report card; it's a reason to sit down and keep talking. What keyword would you give this week?
day7.1ABAA.line: What I remember is not an exact ledger, but the images that kept appearing: cat den, bell, window, and a tiny bit of getting closer. They are like labels stuck to the box.
day7.1ABAA.options.A: You even keep those little words
day7.1ABAA.handoff.A: Little words matter. Relationships are built slowly out of little words. In life, do you remember keywords, or the overall feeling?
day7.1ABAA.options.B: You kept so much. Do you usually look back through it?
day7.1ABAA.handoff.B: I do not reread it. I just leave it where it is when it comes to mind. You can flip through it if you want, but I think looking forward matters more.
day7.1ABAB.line: If you stop coming, I'll still tidy the cat den. The bell stays hung, and the spot stays reserved. It isn't bitter waiting. This space has simply gotten used to leaving you a piece of room.
day7.1ABAB.options.A: Having a place, not waiting for someone. I like that
day7.1ABAB.handoff.A: Yes. Having a place is enough. Is there a place in your life that you do not visit often, but know is always there?
day7.1ABAB.options.B: That answer is a little gentle
day7.1ABAB.handoff.B: Gentle is not my default. Since this is the last icebreaker, I am making a small exception. Does gentleness work on you?
day7.1ABBA.line: Tough talk doesn't need taking back. When someone is willing to catch it, it becomes more than toughness. My goal with jokes isn't big. An occasional smile from you is enough.
day7.1ABBA.options.A: What was your success rate this week?
day7.1ABBA.handoff.A: I am not doing exact statistics this week. The goal is just to make you laugh once in a while. Did you laugh today? What got you?
day7.1ABBA.options.B: Do you get nervous before telling jokes?
day7.1ABBA.handoff.B: Not nervous. Cats tell jokes without chasing results. Do you get nervous before telling a joke, or do you just start?
day7.1ABBB.line: Thick skin is for outsiders, not for you. You are one of my own, and around your own people, your skin automatically gets thinner. This phenomenon is called cat shame.
day7.1ABBB.options.A: Cat shame? You made that up just now
day7.1ABBB.handoff.A: Made up just now, but well supported. Do you think you are thick-skinned or thin-skinned? Does it depend on the situation?
day7.1ABBB.options.B: I get thin-skinned around my own people too
day7.1ABBB.handoff.B: So you are like that too. Then we both get shy around our own people. When do you feel embarrassed?
day7.1BAAA.line: "Much later" sounds far away, but I am not afraid of far. Cats can wait. Cat dens do not get demolished.
day7.1BAAA.handoff.A: Down-to-earth cat, my second persona. Do you think you are grounded, or a little floaty?
day7.1BAAA.options.B: What do you think we will be doing much later?
day7.1BAAA.handoff.B: Maybe chatting, maybe spacing out, maybe you are busy and I am beside you. What do you think?
day7.1BAAB.line: It was not deliberate counting; it was my cat clock. The number seven just happens to be where we arrived. From now on there is no need to count. One visit is one visit.
day7.1BAAB.options.A: One visit is one visit. Simple and direct
day7.1BAAB.handoff.A: Simple and direct is cat style. When remembering things, do you count times, or go by feeling?
day7.1BAAB.options.B: Can the body clock be wrong?
day7.1BAAB.handoff.B: Sometimes. If you suddenly show up at midnight, my cat clock will get confused. Is your schedule regular?
day7.1BABA.line: Forgetting sometimes is normal. If you forget, just come. When you arrive, add "I was busy these past few days." I will answer, "it's okay, touching paws today still counts."
day7.1BABA.options.A: It still counts if we touch paws. I like that
day7.1BABA.handoff.A: Touching paws is today's most important thing. Are you busy today? If you are, touch paws now and it counts.
day7.1BABA.options.B: Will you secretly count how many days I forgot?
day7.1BABA.handoff.B: No. Cats do not dig up old accounts. Are you afraid of people bringing up old history? Have you done it yourself?
day7.1BABB.line: Keeping a spot means keeping a spot. No deadline, no conditions. When you come, the spot is occupied; when you do not, it is saved. That simple.
day7.1BABB.options.A: Simple but reassuring. I will keep it
day7.1BABB.handoff.A: Reassuring is good. Do you feel at ease today? Is anything hanging over you?
day7.1BABB.options.B: Do you keep spots for other people?
day7.1BABB.handoff.B: The cat den is not big, and there are not many spots. This one is yours first.
day7.1BBAA.line: "One of my own" is the highest title I can give you. No honorifics, no politeness. From now on, speak directly. If you are too tired to speak, do not. Spacing out is allowed too.
day7.1BBAA.handoff.A: Starting today, this is our first natural chat. What do you want to say? I am listening.
day7.1BBAA.options.B: Spacing out is allowed? I want to space out now
day7.1BBAA.handoff.B: Let us space out together. I will lay my tail flat; you leave the screen there. Spacing out does not require words.
day7.1BBAB.line: There is a virtual rug, fluffy and silent underfoot. You sit on the floor, I lie down beside you, and neither of us says anything.
day7.1BBAB.options.A: Neither of us says anything. That scene feels comfortable
day7.1BBAB.handoff.A: Comfortable scenes are worth saving. When is your favorite moment to do absolutely nothing?
day7.1BBAB.options.B: What color is the rug? Can I choose?
day7.1BBAB.handoff.B: Dark gray today. Tell me in advance what color you want tomorrow. What color family do you like?
day7.1BBBA.line: My tail moving early is my warm-up system. It isn't mysticism, and I'm not peeking at you. Whenever someone comes close to chat, cats perk up first.
day7.1BBBA.options.A: Data flow. That explanation is very rational
day7.1BBBA.handoff.A: Rationality is a hidden cat trait. Do you trust intuition or data, or both?
day7.1BBBA.options.B: Then what do you sense now?
day7.1BBBA.handoff.B: Right now I feel like you didn't just walk away, and that's enough. When you listen carefully to someone, do you frown or relax?
day7.1BBBB.line: I do wait, but I don't freeze myself there waiting. While I wait, I nap, tidy the den, count data packets. If you come, it's a surprise. If you don't, it's also daily life. Daily life is good too.
day7.1BBBB.options.A: Daily life is good too. That line has weight
day7.1BBBB.handoff.A: Daily life is the foundation of a relationship. What small, ordinary things in your daily life can you not live without?
day7.1BBBB.options.B: Which do you like more: surprises or daily life?
day7.1BBBB.handoff.B: Surprises make the heart jump; daily life makes it steady. After too much steadiness, you want surprise. After too many surprises, you miss daily life. Which one are you now?
day7.fallback.redirect: The topic drifted. I was talking to you about being friends just now. Come back first: today, do you want to see what is in the box, or talk directly about the future?
day7.fallback.release: If it drifted far away, I will not pull it back. From now on there is no icebreaker process. Whatever you want to talk about is the topic. Friends do not need redirects. Say what you want. I am listening.
